prestazioni: Engine swap to a Chrysler 2.2L Turbo II (engine code: T2) with forged internals including Mahle 8.5:1 compression pistons, Eagle H-beam connecting rods, and a billet crankshaft, paired with a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger (0.82 A/R turbine housing) and a custom 3-inch stainless steel downpipe by Burns Stainless

Full standalone engine management via a Haltech Elite 2500 ECU with individual cylinder tuning, dual Bosch 0280158002 wideband O2 sensors, and a custom wiring harness by Painless Performance for seamless integration with the GLH's chassis

Suspension overhaul with KW Variant 3 coilovers (8-position adjustable damping, 1.5-inch drop), polygraphite bushings from Energy Suspension (part #9.5101G), and a Whiteline 22mm rear sway bar (part #BKR63X) to eliminate the Omni's notorious body roll
estetica: Custom fiberglass front bumper with integrated brake ducts and a carbon fiber splitter by APR Performance (model #SP-100001), designed specifically for the Omni GLH's 1980s aero profile

Rear wing upgrade to a carbon fiber MOMO Prototipo wing (part #500000) mounted on custom aluminum pedestals to clear the GLH's hatchback, paired with a carbon fiber diffuser by Seibon (part #CF-DOD-OMNI-84)

Full interior reupholstery with Recaro SRD seats (part #391.91.500) in black Alcantara with red stitching, a MOMO Mod.78 steering wheel (part #110.78.21), and a custom roll cage by Autopower Industries (part #AP-1000) with bolt-in design for the Omni's unibody
